title: Ricinocarpus cordovensis
uses: Ricinocarpus cordovensis has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. It is often used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Ricinocarpus cordovensis is considered a valuable resource in natural remedies due to its adaptogenic properties, which help the body adapt to stress. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
taxonomy: Ricinocarpus cordovensis is classified as a species within the family Salicaceae. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and its genetic similarity to other species in the same family. Ricinocarpus cordovensis is a member of the order Malpighiales, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. The species is also part of the subclass Rosidae, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Further classification of Ricinocarpus cordovensis may include its placement within a particular genus or tribe within the Salicaceae family.

side_effects: Ricinocarpus cordovensis is a plant, but it is not well-documented in scientific literature.
